str:translations.str:about_feeds: This is a web feed, also known as an Atom feed. Subscribe by copying the URL from the address bar into your newsreader. Visit About Feeds to learn more and get started. It's free.

feed.title: ~/nbaig-dev - remote-working
feed.subtitle: I am a backend web developer with 14+ years of experience. In this blog I will be posting my journey with the Rust Programming Language.

entry.title: Overcoming the Challenges of Being a Developer in a Flexible Environment
entry.summary: In today's tech-driven world, flexibility has become a highly sought-after feature in the workplace. As software development continues to evolve at an unprecedented pace, developers are increasingly working in flexible environments that offer them the freedom to choose their own schedules and workspaces. While this newfound autonomy presents many benefits, it also introduces unique challenges that require developers to adapt and develop new skills. In this blog post, we'll explore ten strategies for overcoming the challenges of being a developer in a flexible environment. From establishing boundaries and creating a dedicated workspace to staying accountable, practicing self-care, and fostering trust with your manager, these tips are designed to help you navigate the ever-evolving world of software development with confidence and success. Whether you're new to the field or a seasoned professional, we hope that these strategies will provide valuable insights and practical advice that can help you thrive in a flexible environment. As developers, we're constantly learning and growing, and by sharing our experiences and knowledge with each other, we can continue to push the boundaries of what's possible in our field. Join us on this journey and let's see where it takes us!

entry.title: Overcoming the Challenges of Being a Developer in a Non-English Speaking Country
entry.summary: In a rapidly evolving technology landscape, software development has become one of the most sought-after professions globally. However, not all countries prioritize the tech industry equally, leading to unique challenges for developers working in non-English speaking regions. In this blog post, we'll explore ten ways to overcome these obstacles and thrive as a developer in a non-English speaking country. The post covers topics such as learning the local language, embracing cultural differences, networking, learning local business practices, collaborating with international clients, participating in online communities, attending conferences, leveraging translation tools, working remotely, and being patient. By following these guidelines, developers can succeed and make a meaningful contribution to the tech industry, irrespective of their location or language proficiency.

entry.title: Overcoming the Challenges of Being a Freelance Developer
entry.summary: In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, freelance development has become an increasingly popular career path for many tech-savvy individuals seeking independence and flexibility in their work. However, being a successful freelancer isn't without its challenges, from finding consistent work to managing finances, dealing with rejection, and maintaining a healthy work-life balance. In this blog post, we explore these obstacles and provide practical tips for overcoming them. From pricing your services to building an effective portfolio and learning new skills, our expert insights offer valuable advice that can help you thrive as an independent developer. Whether you're just starting out or a seasoned pro looking to hone your skills, this post is packed with actionable insights that will empower you to succeed in the freelance development space!
